message:
v0.3.14: Fix scanner to never treat git project roots as deletion candidates

The artifact scanner's depth-1 directory walk had a logic error where
git repo root directories (e.g., /data/projects/myproject) could be
classified as artifacts and scored for deletion if they happened to match
a pattern, because the .git check ran after classification and only
skipped scoring — but still let earlier classification proceed.

Rewritten logic:
- Check for .git membership first (cached set + filesystem probe),
  caching the result for future iterations
- Project roots (is_git_repo=true) are unconditionally excluded from
  the to_score list, so they can never become deletion candidates
- Depth-2 child scanning (target/, node_modules/, etc.) still proceeds
  inside project roots, correctly identifying nested build artifacts
- Early-continue moved to after to_score is fully built, avoiding
  skipping depth-2 children when depth-1 itself is not an artifact

Also bumps version 0.3.13 → 0.3.14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
